llm_values.evidence: The agent's failure is primarily due to an extremely inefficient strategy (R4) and repeated outcome misinterpretation (R11). From steps 5 to 13, it spends 9 steps manually scrolling to the end of the document, a task that could be done in 1 step with Ctrl+End. From steps 14 to 26, it spends 13 steps attempting to format the 'References' heading, struggling with text selection (taking 7 steps to select one word) and failing to recognize when its clicks on the 'Bold' button didn't register (Step 21, 25). This pattern of slow, granular GUI interactions and hallucinating success (e.g., in Step 48, where it believes it de-italicized text that remains italicized) causes it to hit the 50-step limit while having only partially addressed the first of nine references.
llm_values.correction: Use more efficient navigation and selection methods. Instead of manual scrolling with the mouse, use keyboard shortcuts like Ctrl+End to navigate to the end of the document. Use triple-click or keyboard shortcuts (Ctrl+Shift+Down) to select entire paragraphs for formatting. Most importantly, use the 'Find and Replace' tool for all systematic changes (like 'ISSS' to 'ISSN') and carefully verify that each formatting action (like Bold or Italic) has actually taken effect before proceeding, to avoid wasting steps on failed interactions.

human_values.evidence: In Step 9, the agent clicks the Italic button to remove italics. In Step 10, looking at the still-highlighted text, the agent incorrectly reasons: 'I see the heading is still selected and italicized.' In reality, the formatting change likely succeeded (or was pending desselection to visually update), but the highlight obscured the actual text rendering. This failure to deselect and properly verify causes the agent to spend the next 14 steps (Steps 10-24) repeatedly trying different methods. It is only in Step 23, when it accidentally clicks away and deselects the text, that it finally realizes the italics have been removed.
human_values.correction: When modifying text formatting (such as removing italics or adding bold), the agent must click on a blank space in the document to deselect the text before visually verifying the result. Relying solely on the appearance of highlighted text often leads to misinterpreting the formatting state due to the visual interference of the selection color.
